artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UAM BIP listing confirms the title, publication date, and PDF link for Rector order 426/2023/2024.
Official UAM guidance page for generative AI use in thesis preparation.
Official UAM guidance page describing levels of generative AI use control in classroom/coursework contexts.
Official UAM recommendation page for generative AI in research work. Treated as guidance, not a binding student policy.
Official UAM FAQ confirms the Rector order as the act regulating AI in education.

artifacts.summary: Discovery found official UAM-wide AI materials: Rector order 426/2023/2024, a central SI page, and UAM recommendation pages for theses, classroom work, and research. One institute-level AI page was rejected for university-wide extraction.

stopConditions: Stop after official UAM-wide Rector order and linked central SI recommendation pages have been fetched.
Do not use institute-specific pages for university-wide claims.
Do not crawl login-only SharePoint intranet content.

artifacts.claimText: Adam Mickiewicz University has Rector order 426/2023/2024 setting rules for student use of AI systems in education, including theses and coursework/assessment work.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: § 1 Zarządzenie określa zasady korzystania z systemów sztucznej inteligencji (systemy SI) w procesie kształcenia studentów, w szczególności w przygotowaniu pracy dyplomowej oraz prac etapowych i zaliczeniowych.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Section 1 says the order sets rules for using AI systems in student education, especially thesis preparation and coursework/assessment work.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For diploma theses, AMU states that before using generative AI systems a student should consult the goals, methods, and scope of use with the thesis supervisor.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Przed wykorzystaniem systemów generujących treści w ramach pracy dyplomowej, student powinien skonsultować cele, sposoby oraz zakres ich wykorzystania z opiekunem naukowym.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Before using content-generating systems in a thesis, the student should consult the goals, methods, and scope with the academic supervisor.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Dopuszczalność wykorzystania w pracy dyplomowej, item 1
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For classroom/course work, AMU recommends that the course coordinator choose the appropriate level of AI-use control based on the syllabus, and that the instructor inform students about permission or prohibition at the first class.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Decyzję o wyborze odpowiedniego poziomu podejmuje koordynator przedmiotu w oparciu o treści i efekty uczenia się zapisane w obowiązującym sylabusie przedmiotu. Osoba prowadząca zajęcia powinna poinformować studentów stosownie o możliwości, bądź zakazie, korzystania z systemów generatywnej SI na pierwszych zajęciach
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The course coordinator chooses the AI-use control level based on the syllabus, and the instructor should inform students at the first class whether generative AI use is permitted or prohibited.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: AMU classroom guidance says that, in course contexts where permission or marking is required, use of generative AI tools without permission or without appropriate marking is treated in the same way as plagiarism.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Użycie takich narzędzi bez zgody prowadzącego, w jakiejkolwiek części pracy przygotowanej w ramach tego przedmiotu, będzie traktowane w taki sam sposób jak plagiat. [...] Użycie takich narzędzi bez odpowiedniego oznaczenia, w jakiejkolwiek części pracy przygotowanej w ramach tego przedmiotu, będzie traktowane w taki sam sposób jak plagiat.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Use without instructor permission, or without appropriate marking where marking is required, is treated the same way as plagiarism for work prepared within that course.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Sections: Używanie dozwolone tylko po uzyskaniu zgody; Użycie dozwolone tylko z odpowiednim oznaczeniem
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: AMU research guidance warns that using online generative AI usually sends prompt information to the service provider, and recommends anonymizing content such as names, places, dates, and other person-identifying fragments.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Konwersacja z systemem GSI wiąże się zazwyczaj z przesłaniem do usługodawcy całości informacji, które uwzględnimy w poleceniu (promptcie), w tym również plików graficznych, wideo, głosowych i innych. Z tego powodu powinniśmy zadbać o odpowiednią anonimizację treści, np. poprzez usunięcie nazw indywidualnych (imion, miejsc), dat i innych fragmentów wskazujących na konkretną osobę.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guidance says online GAI conversations usually send prompt information to the provider, so users should anonymize content by removing names, places, dates, and other person-identifying fragments.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

Adama Mickiewicza w Poznaniu z dnia 20 marca 2024 roku w sprawie zasad korzystania z systemów sztucznej inteligencji w procesie kształcenia studentów
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The FAQ says Rector order 426/2023/2024 is the act regulating AI matters in education at UAM.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a staged machine-reviewed candidate. Claim is scoped to existence and subject matter of Rector order 426/2023/2024 and does not publish canonical status.
Approved as a staged candidate because the claim follows the official UAM thesis recommendation wording and uses should rather than must.
Approved as staged guidance claim; it is limited to course/class contexts and does not infer a single universal permission model.
Approved as a staged academic-integrity candidate because the source directly says unauthorized or unmarked use is treated the same way as plagiarism in the relevant course work.
Approved as staged guidance only. The source is a research recommendation page, so the claim is not framed as a binding student policy.
Approved as source-status corroboration. The FAQ directly identifies the Rector order as the act regulating AI in education.

artifacts.title: Adam Mickiewicz University, Poznań AI Policy Evidence
artifacts.summary: Official UAM sources include Rector order 426/2023/2024 governing student use of AI systems in education and central UAM recommendation pages for theses, classroom work, and research. The staged claims preserve Polish evidence snippets and remain agent-reviewed candidates only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The SharePoint intranet AI page was not crawled because it requires login.
Institute-level AI rules were not used for university-wide claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
